ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Singapore kopitiam-style deep-fried French toast. Closest parent is generic French toast. FoodOn does not capture the deep-frying method, kaya topping, or kopitiam cultural context.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — closest FoodEx2 parent for egg-soaked fried bread; no specific code for kopitiam French toast exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (bread and similar products) used as closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code for deep-fried egg-soaked bread with kaya and butter.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Singapore kopitiam French toast. SNOMED CT does not catalogue regional prepared breakfast dishes at this specificity.

preparation_method: topped with kaya (coconut pandan jam) and butter

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard kopitiam serving is 2 slices of deep-fried egg-soaked white bread (~160g), served with kaya and a pat of butter, providing approximately 424 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: French Toast Kopitiam Style — Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB / Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for kopitiam-style French toast. Values represent the complete dish as served: thick white bread soaked in egg, deep-fried, topped with kaya and butter. Per-100g values preserved from verified input record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread and composite GI estimation for kaya-topped deep-fried bread
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~72 estimated from white bread GI reference range of 70–75 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021). Kaya (coconut pandan jam) adds additional rapidly-absorbed sugars, supporting GI at the upper end of the white bread range. Deep-frying fat moderates postprandial glucose response marginally. GL per 160g serving = (72 × 31g carbohydrate per 100g × 1.6) / 100 = 35.6. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 220 mg/100g; 352 mg per 160g serving — below the 400 mg threshold for a single breakfast item
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content is relatively low for a kopitiam breakfast item at ~352 mg per serving; individuals with hypertension can consume this occasionally without significant sodium concern, but should avoid adding extra soy sauce or salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~72 (high), GL ~35.6 per 160g serving — white bread base combined with kaya sugar creates a high glycaemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: This is a high-GL breakfast (GL ~35.6 per serving) that causes a significant postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional food only, pair it with protein such as soft-boiled eggs to moderate glucose response, and avoid additional kaya.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.5 g/100g (7.2 g per serving) and cholesterol 90 mg/100g (144 mg per serving) from egg, butter, and deep-frying oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (~7.2 g/serving) from butter and absorbed frying oil, together with dietary cholesterol (~144 mg/serving) from egg, warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease; limit to occasional consumption and balance with lower-fat meals throughout the day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Singapore Kopitiam French Toast is a beloved traditional breakfast dish served at coffee shops (kopitiams) across Singapore. Thick slices of white bread are soaked in beaten egg and deep-fried in oil until golden, puffed, and crispy on the outside — a preparation that distinguishes it fundamentally from Western pan-fried French toast. The deep-frying step absorbs approximately 15g of oil per 160g serving, making this dish significantly higher in fat and calories than its Western counterpart. It is served with a generous spread of kaya (a sweet, fragrant jam made from coconut milk, eggs, sugar, and pandan leaf) and a cold pat of salted butter that melts into the hot toast. The flavour profile is rich, eggy, slightly sweet from the kaya, and buttery, with a contrast between the crispy exterior and soft, pillowy interior. Per 2-slice serving (~160g): approximately 424 kcal, 22.4g fat (7.2g saturated), 49.6g carbohydrate, 9.6g protein, 8g sugar, and 352mg sodium. GI ~72 (high), GL ~35.6 per serving. Clinically, this is a high-glycaemic breakfast that should be consumed occasionally by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. It is typically ordered alongside two soft-boiled eggs and a cup of kopi (local coffee), forming the classic Singapore kopitiam breakfast set. Vegetarian and halal-suitable.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database, the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The food is a well-documented kopitiam staple with consistent preparation across Singapore. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference (GI 70–75), which is the closest approved analogous source. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for kopitiam-style deep-fried French toast is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026-04-13. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source for this preparation. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no vitamin C-containing ingredients in this dish). GI is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI 70–75 from the University of Sydney GI Database; kaya sugar contribution supports placement at GI ~72. GL per 160g serving calculated as (72 × 31 × 1.6) / 100 = 35.6. FoodEx2 code A000E used as closest defensible parent (bread and similar products); no specific FoodEx2 code exists for kopitiam deep-fried French toast. No FoodOn URI or SNOMED CT code exists for this dish at the required specificity.
